title: Welcome to Lucerne
desc: The full circle — you welcome a stranger
setting: At the station, a newly-arrived stranger with a suitcase asks you, in English, for help. This is where your own story began.

steps.speaker: Fremde
steps.es: Excuse me, do you speak English? I'm completely lost.
steps.en: Excuse me, do you speak English? I'm completely lost.

options.es: Ja, klar. Aber probier's ruhig zerscht mit Grüezi.
options.en: Yes, of course. But go ahead and try Grüezi first.
options.feedback: Kind and knowing — you give help and a first word at once, just as you once received.
Helpful, but offering that first local word is the gift that keeps giving.
You remember this exact moment from the other side. Spare them thirty seconds.

steps.speaker: Fremde
steps.es: Grüezi...? Did I say that right? I just got here today.
steps.en: Grüezi...? Did I say that right? I just got here today.

options.es: Perfekt gseit! Und kei Sorg, am erste Tag isch alli verlore.
options.en: Said perfectly! And don't worry, on the first day everyone is lost.
options.feedback: Encouraging and reassuring — you turn their fear into a first small win.

options.en: Just like that. You learn fast.
options.feedback: Warm and confidence-building. Exactly what a frightened newcomer needs.
True but flat — a little more warmth would light them up.

steps.speaker: Fremde
steps.es: Thank you. Honestly, it's overwhelming. How long until it feels normal?
steps.en: Thank you. Honestly, it's overwhelming. How long until it feels normal?

options.es: Es bruucht Ziit. Aber eines Tags schaltet niemmer meh uf Änglisch um. Versproche.
options.en: It takes time. But one day nobody switches to English with you anymore. I promise.
options.feedback: You hand them the very horizon you once chased. A quietly perfect thing to say.

options.es: Langsamer als d'wottsch, schnäller als d'dänksch.
options.en: Slower than you want, faster than you think.
options.feedback: Wise, warm, and a little poetic — they'll carry that line.

options.en: No idea, it's different for everyone.
options.feedback: True, but you have real hope to offer — don't shrug it off.

steps.speaker: Fremde
steps.es: That helps more than you know. Thank you so much.
steps.en: That helps more than you know. Thank you so much.

options.es: Gärn gscheh. Willkomme z'Luzärn. Da chunnt's guet.
options.en: You're very welcome. Welcome to Lucerne. It'll turn out fine here.
options.feedback: The exact words from the start of your own story, now in your mouth. The circle is complete.

options.es: Viel Glück uf dim Wäg. Du schaffsch das.
options.en: Good luck on your journey. You'll make it.
options.feedback: Generous and sure — you send them off the way someone once sent you.
A warmer farewell would honour the moment — this is bigger than a quick goodbye.
